name: Tea-Smoked Game Hen
recipeIngredient: Freshly ground black pepper; to taste
2 tablespoons Chinese rice wine OR Dry sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the bird in half using poultry shears or a Chinese cleaver.
2. Rub the halves with salt and pepper, wine, and sesame oil. Allow to marinate for 2 hours.
3. Dip the halves into boiling water for 1 minute. Remove and place on a rack for drying.
4. Smoke the halves in a Cameron stove-top smoker using the tea leaves and Alder or hickory sawdust for 15 minutes.
5. Place the halves on a plate and steam in a bamboo steamer for 20 minutes.
6. Allow the pieces to cool, then deep-fry in hot peanut oil at 375°F until golden brown and crispy, about 4 to 5 minutes.
This recipe serves 3 to 4 as part of a Chinese meal.
